description: San Diego County Employees Retirement Association (SDCERA) — defined benefit pension plans under the California County Employees Retirement Law of 1937 (CERL), covering General and Safety members across 9 benefit tiers
source: SDCERA GASB 68 Actuarial Valuation (FY2021), 2024 ACFR, SDCERA FAQ and member resources

system_overview.name: San Diego County Employees Retirement Association
system_overview.governing_law: California County Employees Retirement Law of 1937 (CERL)
participating_employers: San Diego Local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCO)
San Dieguito River Park Joint Powers Authority
San Diego County Office of Education
Superior Court of California — County of San Diego
system_overview.address: 2275 Rio Bonito Way, Suite 200, San Diego, CA 92108

membership_rules.eligibility: Appointed to permanent position, working at least half-time (20+ hours/week)
membership_rules.effective_date: First full payroll period in the month following appointment
membership_rules.excluded: Part-time under 20 hours/week, emergency, temporary, hourly, provisional, seasonal employees
membership_rules.safety_membership: Active law enforcement, court service officers (added 1998), probation officers (added 1999)

membership_dates.description: Members hired before March 8, 2002 who made irrevocable election to opt out of General Tier A
membership_dates.note: Also includes General members in deferred status on March 8, 2002 who opted out

benefit_formula.name: 2.62% at 62
benefit_formula.description: Age factor increases with age at retirement; maximum 2.62% per year of service at age 62

final_average_compensation.description: Highest 26 consecutive pay periods of earnings

membership_dates.description: Members hired March 8, 2002 to August 27, 2009, plus Tier I and Tier II members converted to Tier A on March 8, 2002

benefit_formula.name: 3.0% at 60
benefit_formula.description: Age factor increases with age; maximum 3.0% per year of service at age 60

final_average_compensation.description: Highest 26 consecutive pay periods of earnings

benefit_formula.name: 2.62% at 62
benefit_formula.description: Same formula as General Tier I; age factor increases with age at retirement

final_average_compensation.description: Highest 78 consecutive pay periods of earnings

membership_dates.description: General members hired January 1, 2013 to June 30, 2018 (PEPRA)

benefit_formula.name: 2.5% at 67
benefit_formula.description: PEPRA formula; age factor increases with age, maximum 2.5% per year at age 67

final_average_compensation.description: Highest 78 consecutive pay periods of earnings

general_tier_C.compensation_limit_note: PEPRA limit: 100% of 2013 Social Security taxable wage base, adjusted for CPI-U

membership_dates.description: General members hired on or after July 1, 2018

benefit_formula.name: 1.62% at 65
benefit_formula.description: Lower-tier 1937 Act formula; age factor increases with age, maximum 1.62% per year at age 65

final_average_compensation.description: Highest 78 consecutive pay periods of earnings

general_tier_D.compensation_limit_note: PEPRA limits apply per Gov Code §7522 et seq.

benefit_formula.name: 3.0% at 50
benefit_formula.description: Age factor reaches maximum 3.0% per year of service at age 50

final_average_compensation.description: Highest 26 consecutive pay periods of earnings

safety_tier_A.irc_415b_sheriff_exception: 15+ years Sheriff service exempts from early retirement reduction

membership_dates.description: Safety members hired August 28, 2009 to December 31, 2012

benefit_formula.name: 3.0% at 55
benefit_formula.description: Age factor reaches maximum 3.0% per year of service at age 55

final_average_compensation.description: Highest 78 consecutive pay periods of earnings

membership_dates.description: Safety members hired January 1, 2013 to June 30, 2020 (PEPRA)

benefit_formula.name: 2.7% at 57
benefit_formula.description: PEPRA safety formula; age factor reaches maximum 2.7% per year at age 57

final_average_compensation.description: Highest 78 consecutive pay periods of earnings

safety_tier_C.compensation_limit_note: PEPRA limit: 120% of 2013 Social Security taxable wage base, adjusted for CPI-U

membership_dates.description: Safety members hired on or after July 1, 2020 (PEPRA)

benefit_formula.name: 2.5% at 57
benefit_formula.description: PEPRA safety formula; age factor reaches maximum 2.5% per year at age 57

final_average_compensation.description: Highest 78 consecutive pay periods of earnings

safety_tier_D.compensation_limit_note: PEPRA limit: 120% of 2013 Social Security taxable wage base, adjusted for CPI-U

benefit_options.description: 5 retirement benefit options; most members choose unmodified

options.name: Unmodified
options.description: Highest monthly benefit; 60% continuance to eligible surviving spouse or domestic partner

options.name: Option 1
options.description: Reduced benefit with lump-sum death benefit to beneficiary

options.name: Option 2
options.description: Reduced benefit; 100% of reduced amount continues to named beneficiary for life

options.name: Option 3
options.description: Reduced benefit; 50% of reduced amount continues to named beneficiary for life

options.name: Option 4
options.description: Reduced benefit; actuarially determined percentage continues to named beneficiary for life

benefit_options.surviving_spouse_eligibility: Married to or registered domestic partner with member for at least 1 year prior to retirement effective date

service_credit_purchases.military_lwop_note: Tier I/A/B: may receive credit at no cost; Tier C/D: may purchase interest-free

reciprocity.description: Members may link SDCERA pension with a pension from certain other California public retirement systems
reciprocity.effect_on_entry_age: Entry age in SDCERA member portal reflects age when started at reciprocal agency

sdcera_sponsored.description: SDCERA has closed its sponsored health insurance plans to new enrollments; existing enrollees cannot add dependents or change coverage, only drop

health_insurance_allowance.eligibility: Tier I or Tier II retirees with at least 10 years of service credit
health_insurance_allowance.description: Monthly reimbursement for health, dental, or prescription insurance premiums up to maximum amount

post_retirement_employment.description: Retirees may work part-time for an SDCERA employer without pension impact if under 960 hours/fiscal year, after 180-day wait

notes: General Tier I and B share the same Government Code section (§31676.12) and age factor formula (2.62% at 62) but differ in FAC period, COLA cap, and minimum retirement age
General Tier D (§31676.01, 1.62% at 65) uses a lower 1937 Act formula adopted as cost-saving measure, not a PEPRA formula despite post-2013 implementation
Contribution rates are entry-age-based and vary by individual member; rates are actuarially determined and adopted annually by the Board of Retirement
COLA banking allows accumulation of unused COLA in years when CPI exceeds the tier cap, for future use when CPI is below the cap
The excess benefit plan (funded by County Board of Supervisors) covers Tier I/A/B members whose benefit exceeds IRC §415(b) limits; Tier C/D members are not eligible
